title: Replace fixed rank blending with nested OOF stacking plus conservative sigmoid calibration
summary: Stacked generalization is specifically meant to learn and correct base-model biases in a second space, and scikit-learn notes that sigmoid calibration preserves ranking while isotonic can change ROC-AUC via ties.
rationale: Stacked generalization is specifically meant to learn and correct base-model biases in a second space, and scikit-learn notes that sigmoid calibration preserves ranking while isotonic can change ROC-AUC via ties. Given the current pipeline already has multiple strong but similar predictors, the next gain is likely in a leakage-safe meta-layer that conditions on regime/domain context rather than a single global weight search.
implementation_hint: Build a level-1 dataset from nested OOF predictions of XGB, CatBoost, ranker/expert models, plus a few meta-features such as wet flag, estimated laps left, and adversarial-domain score. Train a very small logistic/CatBoost meta-learner, then apply per-regime sigmoid calibration only if reliability improves; use isotonic only as a diagnostic because AUC can move via ties.
expected_effect: Often yields small but real gains by learning when each model is trustworthy, while keeping the leaderboard-facing ranking stable.
risk: This is the easiest place to leak; it must be nested OOF, not same-fold tuning. Gains may be marginal if the base models remain too correlated.
